lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Thou hast it now: king, Cawdor, Glamis, all,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: As the weird women promised, and, I fear,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Thou play’dst most foully for’t: yet it was said

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: It should not stand in thy posterity,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: But that myself should be the root and father

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Of many kings. If there come truth from them—

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: As upon thee, Macbeth, their speeches shine—

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Why, by the verities on thee made good,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: May they not be my oracles as well,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: And set me up in hope? But hush! no more.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Here’s our chief guest.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: If he had been forgotten,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: It had been as a gap in our great feast,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: And all-thing unbecoming.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: To-night we hold a solemn supper sir,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And I’ll request your presence.

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Let your highness

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Command upon me; to the which my duties

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Are with a most indissoluble tie

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: For ever knit.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Ride you this afternoon?

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Ay, my good lord.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: We should have else desired your good advice,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Which still hath been both grave and prosperous,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: In this day’s council; but we’ll take to-morrow.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Is’t far you ride?

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: As far, my lord, as will fill up the time

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: ’Twixt this and supper: go not my horse the better,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: I must become a borrower of the night

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: For a dark hour or twain.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Fail not our feast.

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: My lord, I will not.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: We hear, our bloody cousins are bestow’d

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: In England and in Ireland, not confessing

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Their cruel parricide, filling their hearers

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: With strange invention: but of that to-morrow,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: When therewithal we shall have cause of state

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Craving us jointly. Hie you to horse: adieu,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Till you return at night. Goes Fleance with you?

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Ay, my good lord: our time does call upon’s.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I wish your horses swift and sure of foot;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And so I do commend you to their backs.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Farewell.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Let every man be master of his time

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Till seven at night: to make society

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The sweeter welcome, we will keep ourself

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Till supper-time alone: while then, God be with you!

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Sirrah, a word with you: attend those men

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Our pleasure?

lines.speaker: ATTENDANT
lines.text: They are, my lord, without the palace gate.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Bring them before us. To be thus is nothing;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: But to be safely thus.—Our fears in Banquo

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Stick deep; and in his royalty of nature

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Reigns that which would be fear’d: ’tis much he dares;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And, to that dauntless temper of his mind,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: He hath a wisdom that doth guide his valour

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: To act in safety. There is none but he

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Whose being I do fear: and, under him,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: My Genius is rebuked; as, it is said,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Mark Antony’s was by Caesar. He chid the sisters

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: When first they put the name of king upon me,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And bade them speak to him: then prophet-like

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: They hail’d him father to a line of kings:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Upon my head they placed a fruitless crown,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And put a barren sceptre in my gripe,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Thence to be wrench’d with an unlineal hand,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: No son of mine succeeding. If’t be so,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: For Banquo’s issue have I filed my mind;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: For them the gracious Duncan have I murder’d;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Put rancours in the vessel of my peace

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Only for them; and mine eternal jewel

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Given to the common enemy of man,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: To make them kings, the seed of Banquo kings!

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Rather than so, come fate into the list,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And champion me to the utterance! Who’s there!

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: It was, so please your highness.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Well then, now

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Have you consider’d of my speeches? Know

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That it was he in the times past which held you

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: So under fortune, which you thought had been

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Our innocent self: this I made good to you

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: In our last conference, pass’d in probation with you,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: How you were borne in hand, how cross’d, the instruments,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Who wrought with them, and all things else that might

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: To half a soul and to a notion crazed

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Say “Thus did Banquo.”

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: You made it known to us.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I did so, and went further, which is now

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Our point of second meeting. Do you find

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Your patience so predominant in your nature

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That you can let this go? Are you so gospell’d

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: To pray for this good man and for his issue,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Whose heavy hand hath bow’d you to the grave

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And beggar’d yours for ever?

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: We are men, my liege.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Ay, in the catalogue ye go for men;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: As hounds and greyhounds, mongrels, spaniels, curs,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Shoughs, water-rugs and demi-wolves, are clept

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: All by the name of dogs: the valued file

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Distinguishes the swift, the slow, the subtle,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The housekeeper, the hunter, every one

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: According to the gift which bounteous nature

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Hath in him closed, whereby he does receive

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Particular addition, from the bill

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That writes them all alike: and so of men.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Now, if you have a station in the file,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Not i’ the worst rank of manhood, say’t;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And I will put that business in your bosoms,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Whose execution takes your enemy off,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Grapples you to the heart and love of us,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Who wear our health but sickly in his life,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Which in his death were perfect.

lines.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
lines.text: I am one, my liege,

lines.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
lines.text: Whom the vile blows and buffets of the world

lines.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
lines.text: Have so incensed that I am reckless what

lines.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
lines.text: I do to spite the world.

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: And I another

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: So weary with disasters, tugg’d with fortune,

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: That I would set my lie on any chance,

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: To mend it, or be rid on’t.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Both of you

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Know Banquo was your enemy.

lines.speaker: BOTH MURDERERS
lines.text: True, my lord.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: So is he mine; and in such bloody distance,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That every minute of his being thrusts

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Against my near’st of life: and though I could

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: With barefaced power sweep him from my sight

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And bid my will avouch it, yet I must not,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: For certain friends that are both his and mine,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Whose loves I may not drop, but wail his fall

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Who I myself struck down; and thence it is,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That I to your assistance do make love,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Masking the business from the common eye

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: For sundry weighty reasons.

lines.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
lines.text: We shall, my lord,

lines.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
lines.text: Perform what you command us.

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: Though our lives—

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Your spirits shine through you. Within this hour at most

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I will advise you where to plant yourselves;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Acquaint you with the perfect spy o’ the time,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The moment on’t; for’t must be done to-night,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And something from the palace; always thought

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That I require a clearness: and with him—

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: To leave no rubs nor botches in the work—

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Fleance his son, that keeps him company,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Whose absence is no less material to me

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Than is his father’s, must embrace the fate

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Of that dark hour. Resolve yourselves apart:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I’ll come to you anon.

lines.speaker: BOTH MURDERERS
lines.text: We are resolved, my lord.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I’ll call upon you straight: abide within.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: It is concluded. Banquo, thy soul’s flight,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: If it find heaven, must find it out to-night.

stage_directions.text: Sennet sounded. Enter Macbeth, as king, Lady Macbeth, as queen, Lennox, Ross, Lords, Ladies, and Attendants.
Exeunt all but Macbeth, and an Attendant.
Now go to the door, and stay there till we call. Exit Attendant. Was it not yesterday we spoke together?

glosses.definition: Banquo's opening — Macbeth has received everything the Witches promised
you cheated to get it
remain in your descendants
wholly improper
don't miss the banquet — Macbeth's forced hospitality
two
to be king is worthless
the regal nobility of Banquo's inherent character
fearless disposition
my guardian spirit is made to feel inferior — a Plutarchan parallel with Antony before Octavius
attendant guardian spirit — a classical Roman concept
a crown that will produce no heirs of his blood
a royal rule that will leave no lineage
not of his direct descent
Macbeth's deepest wound — Banquo's sons will reign, not his
the devil
the Weird Sisters, now called the Fates
public observation
Banquo's son — the boy who must die with him for the prophecy to fail
the murderers' understood term: death, removal

annotations.title: Banquo as the second fear
annotations.body:
Macbeth's soliloquy after Banquo's exit — 'To be thus is nothing, / But to be safely thus' — reveals the irony of his situation: he has the crown and feels nothing. The crown is 'fruitless,' the sceptre 'barren'; the Sisters' promise that Banquo's descendants will rule is the wound that keeps bleeding.

'My Genius is rebuked' — Macbeth invokes Plutarch's *Life of Antony*, where Antony is said to have a lesser genius than Octavius, one that shrinks in the younger man's presence. Shakespeare had dramatized the image in *Antony and Cleopatra* (probably written the next year). Here, Banquo is Macbeth's Octavius: the calmer, more legitimate rival whose presence diminishes him.

The soliloquy crystallizes the play's problem. Murder was supposed to end the anxiety; it has only relocated it. Every murder Macbeth commits from here forward will be an attempt to kill the residue of the previous one.

annotations.title: The hiring of the murderers
annotations.body:
The extended scene in which Macbeth persuades two 'murderers' to kill Banquo and Fleance is usually cut or shortened in performance. It shouldn't be. The scene is the first and clearest demonstration of how Macbeth has begun to rule: by using Iago-like rhetorical manipulation on men below him in rank.

He does not order the killing. He persuades the murderers that Banquo has been their enemy — dishonest, blocking their advancement, responsible for their poverty. The manipulation is intimate and carefully graded. By the end, they believe they have reason to hate Banquo independently of Macbeth's command. They will kill him as volunteers, not as tools.

The scene rhymes with 1.7, where Lady Macbeth manipulated Macbeth into killing Duncan. Macbeth has learned her technique. He will apply it to everyone below him for the rest of the play — until, in 5.3, he can no longer bring himself to talk to servants.
sources: Harold C. Goddard, *The Meaning of Shakespeare*, vol. 2 (1951)

trials.prompt: Why does Banquo's survival torment Macbeth even after he wears the crown?
options: The Sisters promised Banquo's descendants would rule — making Macbeth's crown 'fruitless' and 'barren'
Banquo refuses to eat at the banquet
Plutarch's *Life of Antony* — Antony's guardian spirit shrinks beside Octavius's; Macbeth feels diminished beside Banquo

trials.prompt: Macbeth's persuasion of the murderers shows he has:
options: Learned Lady Macbeth's manipulation technique and is now applying it to men below him
